title: Education managers
description: Determine and formulate policies and provide overall direction of companies or private and public sector organizations within guidelines set up by a board of directors or similar governing body. Plan, direct, or coordinate operational activities at the highest level of management with the help of subordinate executives and staff managers. Communicate and coordinate with management, shareholders, customers, and employees to address sustainability issues. Enact or oversee a corporate sustainability strategy. Plan, direct, or coordinate the operations of public or private sector organizations, overseeing multiple departments or locations. Duties and responsibilities include formulating policies, managing daily operations, and planning the use of materials and human resources, but are too diverse and general in nature to be classified in any one functional area of management or administration, such as personnel, purchasing, or administrative services. Usually manage through subordinate supervisors. Excludes First-Line Supervisors. (Based on 30 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Select, store, order, issue, and inventory classroom equipment, materials, and supplies; control the inventory or distribution of classroom equipment, materials, or supplies.
Provide additional instruction in vocational areas and maintain inventory of instructional equipment, materials, or aids.
Prepare objectives, outlines, or other materials for courses of study, following curriculum guidelines or requirements.

Confer with other staff members to plan and schedule lessons promoting learning, following approved curricula; collaborate with other education personnel to provide inclusive activities or programs for children with disabilities; and collaborate with other teachers or administrators in the development, evaluation, and revision of secondary or preschool programs.
Use computers, audio-visual aids, and other equipment and materials to supplement presentations and present information in audio-visual or interactive formats, using technology.

Administer standardized ability and achievement tests, and interpret results to determine students' strengths and needs; and administer tests to help determine children's developmental levels, needs, or potential.
Attend professional meetings, educational conferences, and teacher training workshops to maintain and improve professional competence.

Plan and supervise class projects, field trips, visits by guest speakers, or other experiential activities, and guide students in learning from those activities; and plan or conduct activities for a balanced program of instruction, demonstration, and work time that provides students with opportunities to observe, question, and investigate.
Attend staff meetings and serve on committees, as required, and develop individual educational plans (IEPs) designed to promote students' educational, physical, or social development.

Provide assistive devices, supportive technology, and assistance accessing facilities, such as restrooms; teach personal development skills, such as goal setting, independence, and self-advocacy; and instruct students in daily living skills required for independent maintenance and self-sufficiency, such as hygiene, safety, and food preparation.
Maintain accurate and complete student records, prepare reports on children and activities, and observe and evaluate students' performance, behavior, social development, and physical health.
Establish and enforce rules for behavior and procedures for maintaining order among students while employing techniques such as behavior modification and positive reinforcement.
Meet with parents and guardians to provide guidance in using community resources, discuss their children's progress, and determine priorities for their children and their resource needs.
Guide and counsel students with adjustments, academic problems, or special academic interests, and meet with other professionals to discuss individual students' needs and progress.

Monitor teachers and teacher assistants to ensure adherence to inclusive special education program requirements, and modify the general education curriculum for students with disabilities based on various instructional techniques and technologies.
Coordinate placement of students with special needs into mainstream classes and develop or implement strategies to meet the needs of students with a variety of disabilities.

Encourage students to explore learning opportunities or persevere with challenging tasks to prepare them for later grades; and establish and communicate clear objectives for all lessons, units, and projects to students.

Instruct students with disabilities in academic subjects, using a variety of techniques, such as phonetics, multisensory learning, or repetition to reinforce learning and meet students' varying needs; employ special educational strategies during instruction to improve the development of sensory- and perceptual-motor skills, language, cognition, and memory.

Organize and supervise games or other recreational activities to promote physical, mental, or social development, and provide students positive feedback to encourage them and help them develop an appreciation for physical education.

Modify the general preschool curriculum for students with disabilities and instruct students using adapted physical education techniques to improve physical fitness, gross motor skills, perceptual motor skills, or sports and game achievement.

Communicate behavioral observations and student progress reports to students, parents, teachers, or administrators; Meet with parents and guardians to discuss their children's progress and to determine priorities for their children and their resource needs.

Establish and maintain standards of behavior to create safe, orderly, and effective environments for learning; Enforce discipline and attendance rules; Establish clear objectives for all lessons, units, and projects, and communicate those objectives to students.
Teach socially acceptable behavior, employing techniques such as behavior modification or positive reinforcement.

Confer with parents, administrators, testing specialists, social workers, or other professionals to develop individual education plans (IEPs); Develop or write Individualized Education Programs (IEPs) for students; Confer with other staff members to plan and schedule lessons promoting learning, following approved curricula.

Administer standardized ability and achievement tests, and interpret results to determine students' strengths and needs; Prepare, administer, and grade tests and assignments to evaluate students' progress.
Control the inventory or distribution of classroom equipment, materials, or supplies; Organize and label materials and display students' work; Prepare materials and classrooms for class activities.

Observe and evaluate students' performance, behavior, social development, and physical health; Meet with parents or guardians to discuss their children's progress, advise them on using community resources, or teach skills for dealing with students' impairments; Counsel and provide guidance to students regarding personal, academic, vocational, or behavioral issues.

Plan and conduct activities for a balanced program of instruction, demonstration, and work time that provides students with opportunities to observe, question, and investigate; Plan and develop instructional methods and content for educational, vocational, or student activity programs.

Supervise, evaluate, and plan assignments for teacher assistants and volunteers; Monitor teachers and teacher assistants to ensure that they adhere to inclusive special education program requirements; Prepare assignments for teacher assistants or volunteers.

Organize and direct committees of specialists, volunteers, and staff to provide technical and advisory assistance for programs; Direct, coordinate, and evaluate the activities of personnel, including support staff engaged in administering academic institutions, departments, or alumni organizations.

Recruit, hire, train, and evaluate primary and supplemental staff; Recruit, hire, train, and terminate departmental personnel; Identify educational, training, or other development opportunities for sustainability employees or volunteers.

Set educational standards and goals, and help establish policies and procedures to carry them out; Prepare and submit budget requests and recommendations, or grant proposals to solicit program funding; Prepare budgets for approval, including those for funding or implementation of programs.

Develop sustainability reports, presentations, or proposals for supplier, employee, academia, media, government, public interest, or other groups; Create and maintain sustainability program documents, such as schedules and budgets.

Monitor and evaluate effectiveness of sustainability programs; Conduct risk assessments related to sustainability and the environment; Develop methodologies to assess the viability or success of sustainability initiatives.

Direct sustainability program operations to ensure compliance with environmental or governmental regulations; Evaluate and approve proposals for sustainability projects, considering factors such as cost effectiveness, technical feasibility, and integration with other initiatives.

Review and approve new programs, or recommend modifications to existing programs, submitting program proposals for school board approval as necessary; Review and interpret government codes, and develop programs to ensure adherence to codes and facility safety, security, and maintenance.

Develop partnerships with businesses, communities, and other organizations to help meet identified educational needs and to provide school-to-work programs; Coordinate placement of students with special needs into mainstream classes; Participate in faculty and college committee activities.
Define allocations of funds for staff, supplies, materials, and equipment, and authorize purchases; Direct or coordinate activities of businesses or departments concerned with budgeting.
Participate in special education-related activities, such as attending meetings and providing support to special educators throughout the district.
Participate in student recruitment, selection, and admission, making admissions recommendations when required to do so.

Serve as liaisons between organizations, shareholders, and outside organizations; prepare or present reports concerning activities, expenses, budgets, or other items affecting businesses or program services; and develop, evaluate, and revise school programs in collaboration with teachers and administrators.

Coordinate the development or implementation of budgetary control systems, recordkeeping systems, or other administrative control processes and direct or coordinate financial or budget activities to fund operations and maximize investments.
Maintain accurate and complete student records as required by laws, district policies, and administrative regulations; and prepare reports on students and activities as required by administration.

Participate in training and development sessions to improve practices; attend professional meetings, educational conferences, or training workshops to improve professional competence; and instruct individuals in career development techniques, such as job search and interview skills.

Communicate students' progress to parents or teachers in written progress reports or in person; counsel students regarding educational issues, such as course selection and study habits; and provide crisis intervention to students when difficult situations occur at schools.

Plan, direct, or coordinate operational activities at the highest level of management; oversee multiple departments or locations; and manage daily operations, including personnel functions such as selection, training, or evaluation.

Identify, develop, or implement intervention strategies, tutoring plans, or individualized education plans (IEPs) for students; and monitor student performance or assist students in academic environments, promoting productivity and learning.

Review financial statements, sales or activity reports to measure productivity or identify cost reduction or program improvement; and prepare or present reports concerning government statutes or rulings affecting the organization.

Plan and promote career and employment-related programs and events, such as career planning presentations, job fairs, and workshops; and collaborate with students, parents, and teachers to assess student needs and develop tutoring plans.

Provide feedback to students using positive reinforcement techniques to encourage, motivate, or build confidence; and counsel individuals or groups to help them understand and overcome personal, social, or behavioral problems affecting their educational situations.

Develop teaching or training materials, such as handouts or study materials; prepare lesson plans or learning modules for tutoring sessions according to students' needs and goals; and review class material with students to enhance their academic understanding.
Attend meetings, educational conferences, and serve on committees, while also establishing or implementing departmental policies and procedures in conjunction with staff members.

Organize tutoring environments to promote learning; provide private instruction to individuals or small groups of students to improve academic performance; and assist students with developing study skills, note-taking skills, and test-taking strategies.

Collaborate with teachers and administrators in the development of educational programs; prepare and facilitate workshops or collaborative projects; and assist students with adjusting to the educational environment.
Plan, direct, and participate in recruitment and enrollment activities; and develop partnerships with employers for internship opportunities for students.

Assess students' needs for assistance and refer them to appropriate services, including mental health or additional vocational training; and provide information on college degree programs and admission requirements.
Conduct follow-up interviews with students to determine if their needs have been met; and address community groups to explain available counseling services.
Supervise students during activities outside the classroom, such as recess, lunch, and field trips; and enforce rules to maintain order in the classroom or other educational settings.

Prepare or implement budgets and strategic, operational, purchasing, or maintenance plans. Develop or implement marketing strategies. Develop relationships and communicate with customers, site managers, developers, land owners, authorities, utility representatives, or residents. Evaluate fitness and wellness programs to determine their effectiveness. Organize and oversee fitness or wellness programs, such as information presentations, blood drives, or training in first aid or cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR). Monitor regulatory affairs trends related to environmental issues. Prepare wind project documentation, including diagrams or layouts. Monitor operations to ensure compliance with applicable health, safety, or hygiene standards. Provide employee training on compliance related topics, policies, or procedures. Communicate regulatory information to multiple departments and ensure that information is interpreted correctly. Verify staff credentials, such as educational and certification requirements.

Respond to customer, public, or media requests for information about wellness programs or services. Schedule guest appointments. Respond to customer inquiries or complaints. Investigate product complaints and prepare documentation and submissions to appropriate regulatory agencies as necessary.

Train staff in regulatory policies or procedures. Train staff in the use or sale of products, programs, or activities. Assess employee performance and suggest ways to improve work. Develop staff service or retail goals and guide staff in goal achievement. Monitor and review paperwork procedures and systems to prevent error-related shortages. Evaluate new software publishing systems and confer with regulatory agencies concerning news or updates on electronic publishing of submissions.

Conduct periodic internal reviews or audits to ensure that compliance procedures are followed. Maintain documentation of compliance activities, such as complaints received or investigation outcomes. Conduct or direct the internal investigation of compliance issues. File appropriate compliance reports with regulatory agencies. Prepare management reports regarding compliance operations and progress. Monitor compliance systems to ensure their effectiveness. Develop regulatory strategies and implementation plans for the preparation and submission of new products.

Develop or implement plans for the sustainable regeneration of brownfield sites to ensure regeneration of a wider area by providing environmental protection or economic and social benefits. Coordinate on-site activities for environmental cleanup or remediation projects to ensure compliance with environmental laws, standards, regulations, or other requirements. Identify environmental contamination sources. Plan or implement brownfield redevelopment projects to ensure safety, quality, and compliance with applicable standards or requirements.

Develop and maintain standard operating procedures or local working practices. Monitor emerging trends regarding industry regulations to determine potential impacts on organizational processes. Keep informed regarding pending industry changes, trends, or best practices. Track cost-containment strategies and programs to evaluate effectiveness. introduce internal policy changes if compliance standards change.

Negotiate contracts for services or materials needed for environmental remediation. Review loss prevention exception reports and cash discrepancies to ensure adherence to guidelines. Prepare or assist in the preparation of applications for environmental, building, or other required permits. Manage wind project costs to stay within budget limits. Direct documentation efforts to ensure compliance with domestic and international regulations and standards. Supervise employees or subcontractors to ensure quality of work or adherence to safety regulations or policies. Review or evaluate proposals or bids to make recommendations regarding awarding of contracts.

Conduct needs assessments or surveys to determine interest in, or satisfaction with, wellness and fitness programs, events, or services. Develop marketing campaigns to promote a healthy lifestyle or participation in fitness or wellness programs.

Identify and apply for project funding. Collaborate with human resources departments to ensure the implementation of consistent disciplinary action strategies in cases of compliance standard violations. Identify compliance issues that require follow-up or investigation. Monitor regulatory affairs activities to ensure their alignment with corporate sustainability or green initiatives. Provide regulatory guidance to departments or development project teams regarding design, development, evaluation, or marketing of products.

Track attendance, participation, or performance data related to wellness events. Maintain client databases. Inventory products and order new supplies. Identify compliance issues that require follow-up or investigation. Review communications such as securities sales advertising to ensure there are no violations of standards or regulations. Report violations of compliance or regulatory standards to duly authorized enforcement agencies as appropriate or required.

Remove or archive obsolete documentation. Conduct quantitative risk assessments for human health, environmental, or other risks. Review or evaluate designs for contaminant treatment or disposal facilities. Conduct feasibility or cost-benefit studies for environmental remediation projects. Verify that all regulatory policies and procedures have been documented, implemented, and communicated. Develop plans for future expansion or improvement based on assessment results.
Propose, review, or approve modifications to project plans, and develop or update project plans including objectives, technologies, schedules, funding, and staffing.
Produce and distribute project documents and create project status presentations for delivery to customers or project personnel.
Prepare and submit budget estimates, progress reports, or cost tracking reports, and monitor costs incurred by project staff to identify budget issues.
Negotiate with project stakeholders or suppliers to obtain resources or materials, and identify, review, or select vendors or consultants to meet project needs.
Monitor the performance of project team members to provide performance feedback, and monitor project milestones and deliverables.
Monitor project needs such as resources, staff, or finances by reviewing project objectives and schedules, and plan, schedule, or coordinate project activities to meet deadlines.
